name: Sun
title: The Sun: Our Solar System's Star
about: The Sun is the heart of our solar system, a massive ball of plasma that provides heat, light, and energy to everything within its gravitational pull. Its immense size and temperature are fueled by nuclear fusion, a process that combines hydrogen atoms into helium, releasing vast amounts of energy. The Sun's magnetic field constantly changes, influencing solar activity like sunspots and solar flares, affecting space weather and potentially disrupting Earth-based technologies.

name: Mercury
title: Mercury: The Closest Planet
about: Mercury is the nearest planet to the Sun and the smallest planet in our solar system. It has almost no atmosphere, causing extreme temperature changes between day and night. Despite its small size, Mercury has a large iron core that generates a weak magnetic field.

name: Venus
title: Venus: Earth's Toxic Twin
about: Venus is often called Earth's twin because of its similar size and composition. However, its atmosphere is filled with carbon dioxide, creating an extreme greenhouse effect that makes it the hottest planet in our solar system. Thick sulfuric acid clouds cover its surface.

name: Earth
title: Earth: Our Blue Marble
about: Earth is the only known planet to support life. It has liquid water, a breathable atmosphere, and a magnetic field that protects living organisms from harmful solar radiation. About 71% of Earth's surface is covered by oceans.

name: Mars
title: Mars: The Red Planet
about: Mars is famous for its reddish appearance caused by iron oxide on its surface. Scientists believe Mars once had rivers and lakes. Today it is one of the most explored planets because it may have supported microbial life billions of years ago.

name: Jupiter
title: Jupiter: The Gas Giant
about: Jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system. It is composed mainly of hydrogen and helium and is famous for the Great Red Spot, a giant storm that has raged for centuries. Jupiter has more than 90 known moons.

name: Saturn
title: Saturn: The Ringed Planet
about: Saturn is best known for its spectacular ring system made of billions of ice and rock particles. It is the second-largest planet in the solar system and has more than 140 moons, including Titan, which has a dense atmosphere.

name: Uranus
title: Uranus: The Tilted Planet
about: Uranus rotates almost on its side, making it unique among the planets. It is an ice giant with a blue-green color caused by methane in its atmosphere. Its unusual tilt creates extreme seasonal changes.

name: Neptune
title: Neptune: The Distant World
about: Neptune is the farthest known planet from the Sun. It is famous for its deep blue color and the strongest winds in the solar system, which can exceed 2,000 km/h. Triton, its largest moon, orbits in the opposite direction of Neptune's rotation.
